verdict: The AI displacement risk for this occupation is moderate, with a score of 83 indicating that while some tasks may be automated, the role still requires significant human judgment and interaction.

reasoning_steps.title: Task Automation
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks such as reading books to classes, preparing materials and classrooms, and observing students' performance can be automated to some extent.

reasoning_steps.title: Human Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: However, tasks that require human judgment, such as guiding and counseling students, adapting teaching methods, and resolving behavioral and academic problems, are less likely to be automated.

adjacent_careers.name: Instructional Designer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Instructional designers create educational materials and develop curricula, which may involve some automation, but also require human judgment and creativity.

adjacent_careers.name: Tutor
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Tutors work one-on-one with students and provide individualized support, which requires human interaction and empathy.

recent_developments.headline: Universities update curricula to assume AI in the loop
recent_developments.impact: Programs add prompt-craft, verification, and AI-direction skills as core competencies.

recent_developments.headline: K-12 districts pilot AI tutors at scale
recent_developments.impact: Khanmigo, MagicSchool, and others reach millions of students with personalized practice.

recent_developments.headline: Teacher workload tools see fast adoption
recent_developments.impact: Lesson planning, rubric drafting, and feedback writing are common AI use-cases in surveys.

quotes.quote: We are at the cusp of using AI for probably the biggest positive transformation that education has ever seen — a personal tutor for every student, on every subject.
quotes.speaker: Sal Khan
quotes.title: Founder, Khan Academy

quotes.quote: If we just use AI to grade essays faster, we have failed. The opportunity is to free teachers from low-value work so they can do what only humans can do — mentor.
quotes.speaker: Linda Darling-Hammond
quotes.title: President, Learning Policy Institute; Stanford emerita

quotes.quote: Teachers are not going to be replaced by AI. Teachers who use AI well are going to replace teachers who do not.
quotes.speaker: Ethan Mollick
quotes.title: Associate Professor, Wharton; author of Co-Intelligence
